title: Salsa on Cotton
stainChemistry: Salsa is tomato + chili oil + onion + citric acid + sometimes cilantro + garlic. The three stain-forming components are: tomato (lycopene + tannin), chili pepper (capsaicin carried in oil + capsanthin red pigment), and onion (sulfur compounds that can darken over time). The capsaicin oil is the real lock — fat-soluble, drives the red pepper pigment deep into cotton. Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, and hot wash. Treatment is oil-first, then tannin, then oxidizer — same oil-then-color pattern as tomato-sauce.
whyThisWorks: Capsaicin + capsanthin behave like mustard's curcumin + carrier oil — the oil protects the pigment until the oil is gone. Dawn / NSD pulls the capsaicin oil. Acid-side tannin spotter breaks the tomato-tannin bond. 3% H₂O₂ oxidizes residual red. Hot wash finishes. Skip the oil strip and the rest plateaus at faint orange.
defaultAssumption: Fresh salsa on cotton is a high-confidence DIY. Heavy-oil salsa (restaurant-style with visible oil film) adds a solvent pre-treatment. Aged salsa is tougher because both the lycopene and capsanthin oxidize deeper.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Dull blade, cold water, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Scrape solid salsa chunks off with a dull blade. Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric. Cold only — hot water drives capsaicin oil deeper into cotton.
Apply NSD, work with a soft brush. Strips the chili oil carrier. Essential first step — peroxide can't reach the pigment while the oil is there.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side tannin spotter by dropper on tomato tannin. Dwell 3–5 min, blot.
3% H₂O₂ on residual orange-red. Oxidizes lycopene + capsanthin. Skip on dark cotton.

steps: 1. Scrape chunks. Cold flush from the back.
3. Acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A).
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Oil off FIRST — peroxide can't work through capsaicin oil
Never hot-flush — drives chili oil deeper

homeSolutions.agent: Scrape + Cold Water From the Back
homeSolutions.instruction: Scrape salsa chunks off with a dull butter knife. Hold the BACK of the fabric under cold running water for a minute. Cold only — hot water drives the chili oil deeper.

Salsa has chili oil that carries the red pepper pigment. That oil has to come off BEFORE anything else can reach the pigment. Work Dawn dish soap into the stain with a fingertip or soft toothbrush, let sit 10 min, rinse cool. The stain should lighten noticeably.
Mix 1 tsp white vinegar with 3 tbsp cool water. Dab on the stain, let sit 3 min, blot. Breaks the tomato tannin bond.
For residual orange-red on white cotton: dab 3% drugstore hydrogen peroxide, let it bubble 3–5 min, blot. Skip on dark cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder on the hottest setting the care label allows. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.

If salsa's been hot-dried or two home rounds haven't cleared it, take to a dry cleaner. Tell them: "Salsa on cotton, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% hydrogen peroxide."

steps: 1. Scrape chunks. Cold water flush from the BACK.
2. Dawn FIRST — strip the chili oil. 10 min dwell, rinse cool.
3. Diluted white vinegar dab for red residue.
4. 3% hydrogen peroxide on residual color (whites only).
5. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if salsa has been hot-dried or two home rounds didn't clear it.
warnings: Oil off FIRST — peroxide blocked by capsaicin oil otherwise
Cold water during spotting — hot drives oil deeper
Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach or ammonia

neverDo: Never hot-flush fresh salsa — drives chili oil deeper
Never skip the Dawn / oil-strip step
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash
Dyed cotton: skip or inside-seam-test peroxide

materialWarnings: Oil carrier off first, color second

escalation.when: Salsa heat-set by iron or dryer, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Salsa on cotton, age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + 3% H₂O₂."

contextQuestions: Oily restaurant-style salsa or lean homemade?
Has it been through a hot wash or dryer?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Salsa + chili oil is tomato sauce plus extra chili pigment. Same oil-first-then-color pattern. Heavy oil restaurant salsa trends harder — more solvent passes.
